ucs.title: [LLM] Putter Panda (APT2 / MSUpdater) Run-key persistence invoking rundll32 against user-writable DLL
ucs.description: Hunts the documented 4H RAT / 3PARA RAT / pngdowner persistence pattern where Putter Panda writes an HKCU/HKLM Run-key value that calls rundll32.exe against a DLL dropped in a user-writable directory (often paired with a stolen-cert signed loader). Combines T1547.001 with T1055.001 and the obfuscated-DLL drop typical of CrowdStrike's MSUpdater cluster.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=false count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where Registry.registry_path IN ("*\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run\\*","*\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\RunOnce\\*","*\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\Explorer\\Run\\*") (Registry.registry_value_data="*rundll32*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*regsvr32*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\Users\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\AppData\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\Temp\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\Public\\*") by Registry.dest Registry.user Registry.process_id Registry.registry_path Registry.registry_value_name Registry.registry_value_data _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)` | rex field=registry_value_data "(?i)(?<dropped_path>(?:[A-Z]:)?\\\\(?:Users|AppData|ProgramData|Temp|Public|Intel|PerfLogs)\\\\[^\"]+\.(?:dll|exe|dat|bin|tmp))" | search dropped_path=* | join type=outer dest dropped_path [| tstats summariesonly=false count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="rundll32.exe" OR Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" by Processes.dest Processes.process Processes.parent_process Processes.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval dropped_path=process] | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | table firstTime lastTime dest user registry_path registry_value_name registry_value_data dropped_path process
ucs.kql:
let _knownInstallers = dynamic(["msiexec.exe","setup.exe","explorer.exe","trustedinstaller.exe","officeclicktorun.exe","onedrivesetup.exe"]);
let _runKeys = dynamic([@"\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run", @"\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\RunOnce", @"\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer\Run"]);
let _userWritable = dynamic([@"\Users\", @"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Temp\", @"\Public\", @"\Intel\", @"\PerfLogs\"]);
DeviceRegistryEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
| where RegistryKey has_any (_runKeys)
| where isnotempty(RegistryValueData)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (_knownInstallers)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| extend RVD = tolower(RegistryValueData)
| where RVD has_any ("rundll32","regsvr32")
   or RVD matches regex @"(?i)\\(users|appdata|programdata|temp|public|intel|perflogs)\\.+\.(dll|exe|dat|bin|tmp)"
| extend DroppedPath = extract(@"(?i)([a-z]:\\(?:Users|AppData|ProgramData|Temp|Public|Intel|PerfLogs)\\[^\"\s,]+\.(?:dll|exe|dat|bin|tmp))", 1, RegistryValueData)
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "rundll32.exe" or FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
    | project DeviceId, ProcTime = Timestamp, ProcCmd = ProcessCommandLine, ParentImg = InitiatingProcessFileName, ChildImg = FileName
  ) on DeviceId
| where isempty(DroppedPath) or ProcCmd has DroppedPath or ProcTime between (Timestamp - 5m .. Timestamp + 1d)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData, DroppedPath, ProcTime, ProcCmd
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _runKeys = dynamic([@"\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run", @"\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\RunOnce", @"\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer\Run"]);
let _knownInstallers = dynamic(["msiexec.exe","setup.exe","explorer.exe","trustedinstaller.exe","officeclicktorun.exe","onedrivesetup.exe"]);
ImRegistryEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventType in ("RegistryKeyCreated","RegistryValueSet")
| where RegistryKey has_any (_runKeys)
| where isnotempty(RegistryValueData)
| where ActingProcessName !in~ (_knownInstallers)
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| extend RVD = tolower(RegistryValueData)
| where RVD has_any ("rundll32","regsvr32")
   or RVD matches regex @"(?i)\\(users|appdata|programdata|temp|public|intel|perflogs)\\.+\.(dll|exe|dat|bin|tmp)"
| extend DroppedPath = extract(@"(?i)([a-z]:\\(?:Users|AppData|ProgramData|Temp|Public|Intel|PerfLogs)\\[^\"\s,]+\.(?:dll|exe|dat|bin|tmp))", 1, RegistryValueData)
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername, ActingProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine, RegistryKey, RegistryValue, RegistryValueData, DroppedPath
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Putter Panda Run-Key Persistence via rundll32 against User-Writable DLL
id: 9f3a8e1c-2b4d-4f5a-9c7e-7a1b2c3d4e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects the Putter Panda (APT2 / MSUpdater) persistence pattern where a Run/RunOnce value is written that invokes rundll32 (or regsvr32) against a DLL/EXE staged in a user-writable directory. Pattern documented in CrowdStrike's Putter Panda report against 3PARA RAT, 4H RAT and pngdowner droppers.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0024/
  - https://www.crowdstrike.com/wp-content/brochures/PutterPanda.pdf
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1547.001
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1055.001
  - attack.g0024
logsource:
  category: registry_set
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_run_key:
    TargetObject|contains:
      - '\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run\'
      - '\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\RunOnce\'
      - '\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer\Run\'
  selection_payload:
    Details|contains:
      - 'rundll32'
      - 'regsvr32'
      - '\Users\'
      - '\AppData\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\Temp\'
      - '\Public\'
      - '\Intel\'
      - '\PerfLogs\'
  filter_installers:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\msiexec.exe'
      - '\setup.exe'
      - '\TrustedInstaller.exe'
      - '\OfficeClickToRun.exe'
      - '\OneDriveSetup.exe'
  condition: selection_run_key and selection_payload and not filter_installers
falsepositives:
  - Niche third-party software installers writing autorun stubs into user-writable paths
  - Developer tooling that registers per-user COM helpers via regsvr32
level: high
ucs.rationale: This is the literal MSUpdater / 4H RAT / 3PARA RAT install pattern documented in the CrowdStrike Putter Panda intelligence report attributing the activity to PLA Unit 61486 (12th Bureau, 3rd GSD): persistence registered under HKCU\...\Run pointing at rundll32 invoking a small encoded DLL dropped to %APPDATA%, %TEMP% or %ProgramData%. Generic Run-key alerts catch persistence broadly; this rule is h

_field_issues.message: `ProcessCommandLine` is not a column of DeviceRegistryEvents; it does exist on DeviceProcessEvents, DeviceEvents, AlertEvidence, ASCDeviceEvents.

`FileName` is not a column of DeviceRegistryEvents; it does exist on DeviceProcessEvents, DeviceFileEvents, DeviceImageLoadEvents, DeviceEvents, EmailAttachmentInfo, AlertEvidence, SecurityEvent, CommonSecurityLog, ThreatIntelligenceIndicator, ImWebSession, ASCDeviceEvents.

ucs.title: [LLM] Putter Panda DLL side-loading: signed binary executing from user-writable path with co-located unsigned DLL
ucs.description: Hunts the Putter Panda / APT2 loader pattern where a legitimately-signed Microsoft / NVIDIA / Intel / security-vendor binary is copied into a user-writable directory and side-loads a malicious DLL placed alongside it (e.g. the MSUpdater trojan family). The pairing of (a) trusted-vendor signed loader, (b) execution from non-Program-Files path, and (c) adjacent non-Microsoft DLL load is the canonical Putter Panda staging signature.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=false count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Intel\\*") (Processes.process_company="Microsoft Corporation" OR Processes.process_company="Adobe Systems Incorporated" OR Processes.process_company="Hewlett-Packard" OR Processes.process_company="Intel Corporation" OR Processes.process_company="NVIDIA Corporation" OR Processes.process_company="McAfee, Inc." OR Processes.process_company="Symantec Corporation" OR Processes.process_company="Kaspersky Lab") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process_hash Processes.process_company Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename process_path as loader_path | join type=inner dest loader_path [| tstats summariesonly=false count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" (Filesystem.file_path="*\\Users\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Temp\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Public\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Intel\\*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_hash Filesystem.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | eval loader_path=replace(file_path, "\\\\[^\\\\]+\.dll$", "")] | where file_hash != process_hash | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | table firstTime lastTime dest user process_name loader_path process_hash process_company file_name file_hash parent_process_name
ucs.kql:
let _trustedVendors = dynamic(["Microsoft Corporation","Microsoft Windows","Adobe Systems Incorporated","Adobe Inc.","Hewlett-Packard","Intel Corporation","NVIDIA Corporation","McAfee, Inc.","Symantec Corporation","Kaspersky Lab","Realtek Semiconductor Corp."]);
let _userWritable = dynamic([@"\Users\", @"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Temp\", @"\Public\", @"\Intel\", @"\PerfLogs\"]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName in~ (_trustedVendors)
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (_userWritable)
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files"
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows"
| where FileName endswith ".dll"
| where SHA256 != InitiatingProcessSHA256
| extend LoaderDir = tolower(InitiatingProcessFolderPath)
| extend DllDir = tolower(FolderPath)
| where DllDir startswith LoaderDir or LoaderDir startswith DllDir
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FileName endswith ".dll"
    | project DeviceId, DllSHA256 = SHA256, DllPath = FolderPath, DllDropProc = InitiatingProcessFileName, DropTime = Timestamp
  ) on $left.DeviceId == $right.DeviceId, $left.SHA256 == $right.DllSHA256
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, LoaderImage = InitiatingProcessFileName, LoaderVendor = InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName, LoaderPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath, LoaderSHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256, LoadedDll = FileName, LoadedDllPath = FolderPath, LoadedDllSHA256 = SHA256, DllDropProc, DropTime
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _trustedVendors = dynamic(["Microsoft Corporation","Microsoft Windows","Adobe Systems Incorporated","Hewlett-Packard","Intel Corporation","NVIDIA Corporation","McAfee, Inc.","Symantec Corporation","Kaspersky Lab","Realtek Semiconductor Corp."]);
let _userWritable = dynamic([@"\Users\", @"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Temp\", @"\Public\", @"\Intel\", @"\PerfLogs\"]);
WindowsEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon"
| where EventID == 7
| extend Image = tostring(EventData.Image),
         ImageLoaded = tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded),
         Signed = tostring(EventData.Signed),
         SignatureStatus = tostring(EventData.SignatureStatus),
         Company = tostring(EventData.Company),
         User = tostring(EventData.User),
         LoaderHash = tostring(EventData.Hashes),
         DllHash = tostring(EventData.Hashes)
| where Company in~ (_trustedVendors)
| where Image has_any (_userWritable)
| where Image !startswith @"C:\Program Files" and Image !startswith @"C:\Windows"
| where ImageLoaded endswith ".dll"
| extend LoaderDir = tolower(strcat_array(array_slice(split(Image, @"\"), 0, -2), @"\")),
         DllDir = tolower(strcat_array(array_slice(split(ImageLoaded, @"\"), 0, -2), @"\"))
| where DllDir startswith LoaderDir or LoaderDir startswith DllDir
| where User !endswith "$"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, User, LoaderImage = Image, LoaderVendor = Company, LoadedDll = ImageLoaded, LoaderHash, DllHash
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: Putter Panda's MSUpdater family and 4H RAT droppers are notorious for abusing legitimately-signed binaries (historically a Microsoft IME helper and security-vendor utilities) by relocating them to %APPDATA% or C:\Intel and dropping a malicious sibling DLL whose name matches an import the loader expects. The CrowdStrike Putter Panda report explicitly documented the side-loading-from-user-path patte
